description: End-to-End Integration Tests + Gate Verification - Create integration test suite that exercises all Phase 24 hard gates: one user account across all 13 apps without re-auth, passkey enrollment + login E2E, permission check <5ms p99, and account deletion purge within 14 days. Also verify full project typecheck, test, build, and lint pass.
steps: 1. Create packages/auth/src/__tests__/phase24-integration.test.ts: Integration test that: (a) Creates a user via QuantMail OAuth provider, (b) Generates auth tokens, (c) Validates the token is accepted by all 13 registered app clients, (d) Verifies single session works cross-app without re-authentication, (e) Tests passkey enrollment flow end-to-end with mocked authenticator, (f) Tests passkey authentication flow with the enrolled credential.

2. Create packages/identity-permissions/src/__tests__/phase24-gates.test.ts: Performance gate tests that: (a) Run can() 1000 times and assert p99 < 5ms, (b) Test permission check across all resource types and roles, (c) Verify consent withdrawal has immediate effect (subsequent hasConsent returns false).

3. Create packages/auth/src/__tests__/account-deletion-gate.test.ts: Test that: (a) Account deletion request sets 14-day scheduled purge, (b) purgeAccount actually removes all data (sessions, tokens, credentials), (c) Cancellation within grace period works.
4. Run full project verification: pnpm typecheck && pnpm test && pnpm build && pnpm lint to confirm all gates pass globally.
5. Fix any cross-package type issues that emerge from the new exports (e.g., packages depending on @quant/common or @quant/auth that may need updated imports).
acceptance_criteria: Integration test proves one user account works across all 13 apps without re-auth
Passkey enrollment + login E2E test passes
Permission check p99 < 5ms verified with 1000-iteration benchmark
Account deletion schedules purge within 14 days and actually clears all data
Full project passes: pnpm typecheck (82+ tasks), pnpm test (84+ tasks), pnpm build, pnpm lint
No regressions in existing test suites
